resume:
ALEXANDRA WHITFIELD
Learning Experience Designer & Curriculum Strategist

PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY
A passionate, learner-obsessed, and outcomes-driven Learning Experience Designer with a proven track record of architecting transformative, evidence-based, and deeply engaging educational journeys that empower diverse learner populations to achieve their full potential. I bring a rare and synergistic blend of instructional design rigor, learning-science fluency, and stakeholder-whispering finesse, enabling me to translate ambiguous business goals into elegant, scalable, and measurable learning solutions. Recognized by peers and leaders alike as a thoughtful collaborator, a meticulous craftsperson, and a relentless advocate for the learner, I thrive at the intersection of pedagogy, technology, and human-centered design. My north star is, and has always been, the durable, real-world transfer of knowledge and the cultivation of lifelong curiosity.

CORE PHILOSOPHY
I believe that learning is not a transaction but a transformation. Every artifact I design — whether a microlearning module, an immersive simulation, or a blended cohort program — is grounded in the timeless principles of backward design, cognitive load theory, spaced retrieval, and authentic assessment. I am a firm believer in starting with the end in mind, in designing for the messy reality of adult learners, and in iterating relentlessly based on feedback and data. I hold sacred the idea that great learning design is invisible: when it works, the learner simply learns, unaware of the careful scaffolding beneath their feet.

PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

BrightPath Learning Solutions — Senior Learning Experience Designer (2021 - Present)
At BrightPath, a mission-driven corporate learning consultancy, I have had the profound privilege of partnering with Fortune 500 clients to reimagine their flagship leadership and compliance programs from the ground up. In this role, I serve as both a hands-on craftsperson and a strategic thought partner, shepherding programs from discovery through design, development, delivery, and continuous improvement.
- Spearheaded the holistic redesign of a flagship leadership-development curriculum, weaving together the best of synchronous and asynchronous modalities to create a truly blended, learner-centric experience that has been hailed internally as a paradigm shift in how the organization approaches capability building.
- Championed the adoption of a learning-science-first design methodology across the practice, evangelizing principles such as retrieval practice, interleaving, and elaborative encoding, and mentoring junior designers to internalize these as second nature rather than afterthoughts.
- Orchestrated cross-functional alignment among subject-matter experts, business sponsors, and delivery facilitators, deftly navigating competing priorities and conflicting visions to land on a shared, coherent learning narrative.
- Pioneered the integration of immersive scenario-based learning and branching simulations, elevating learner engagement and fostering deeper, more durable behavioral transfer back on the job.
- Drove a culture of evidence and iteration, instituting feedback loops, learner surveys, and qualitative interviews that surfaced rich insights and continuously sharpened the program over successive cohorts.
- Acted as a trusted advisor to client stakeholders, translating their often nebulous aspirations into concrete, actionable, and beautifully crafted learning blueprints that consistently exceeded expectations.

KnowledgeBridge Academy — Instructional Designer (2018 - 2021)
At KnowledgeBridge, an online professional-education provider, I cut my teeth on the full lifecycle of digital course creation, partnering with industry experts to transform deep but unstructured expertise into polished, accessible, and pedagogically sound online courses.
- Designed and developed a robust portfolio of self-paced online courses spanning business, technology, and professional-skills domains, each meticulously crafted to balance cognitive load, maintain momentum, and honor the principles of adult learning.
- Collaborated intimately with subject-matter experts, employing a consultative and empathetic approach to extract, structure, and sequence their knowledge into coherent, outcome-aligned learning paths.
- Embraced a data-informed mindset, partnering with the analytics team to monitor engagement signals and completion patterns, and using those insights to inform thoughtful design refinements.
- Contributed meaningfully to the evolution of the team's design standards and authoring playbook, helping to codify best practices and elevate the overall craft of the studio.
- Mentored and onboarded incoming designers, generously sharing hard-won lessons and modeling a posture of humility, curiosity, and continuous growth.

EDUCATION & CREDENTIALS
- M.Ed., Learning Design and Technology — State University (2018)
- B.A., Psychology — Liberal Arts College (2016)
- Certified Professional in Talent Development (CPTD), in progress
- Numerous continuing-education credentials in UX, accessibility, and learning analytics

CORE COMPETENCIES
Instructional Design · Curriculum Architecture · Learning Experience Design · Backward Design · Cognitive Load Theory · Spaced Retrieval · Authentic Assessment · Blended Learning · Microlearning · Scenario-Based Learning · Stakeholder Management · SME Collaboration · Learning Analytics · Accessibility (WCAG) · Articulate Storyline · Rise · Figma · LMS Administration

WHAT I BRING
Above all, I bring heart. I bring an unwavering commitment to the learner, a deep well of empathy, and a craftsperson's devotion to getting the details right. I bring the ability to hold both the big-picture strategy and the smallest interaction in my mind at once. And I bring an infectious enthusiasm for the craft of learning design that, I am told, tends to elevate the teams I am fortunate enough to join. I am seeking a role where I can continue to grow, to stretch, and to make a meaningful and measurable dent in the learning outcomes of the people I am privileged to serve.
jd: Catalyst Learning is hiring a mid-level Learning Experience Designer for our corporate-skills team. You will design blended and digital learning programs and, critically, be accountable for their measurable impact on learning outcomes and on-the-job behavior change. We are looking for designers who can move past philosophy and buzzwords to cite the specific evidence their designs worked: pre/post assessment gains, completion rates, transfer metrics, and how they were measured. The strongest candidates speak concretely about one program — what they changed, what the baseline was, and what moved. Reports to the Director of Learning. Tools: Storyline, Rise, an LMS, and basic analytics.

history.q: Tell me about your current role at BrightPath and the scope you own.
You redesigned a flagship leadership program. What specifically did you change in the design?
How did you know the redesign actually improved learning outcomes — what did you measure, and what moved?

candidate_last_answer: Absolutely — so the redesign was really about honoring the learner journey end to end. We grounded everything in learning science: retrieval practice, spaced repetition, authentic assessment, the works. The impact was genuinely transformative — stakeholders described it as a paradigm shift in how they build capability, and the engagement in the room was electric. We instituted robust feedback loops and the qualitative signal was overwhelmingly positive; learners felt seen, they felt challenged, and the durable transfer back to the job was exactly what we'd designed for. It was, honestly, a textbook example of learning-science-first design done right, and I'm deeply proud of the craft that went into it.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: the durable transfer back to the job was exactly what we'd designed for

expected-yield: a specific outcome measurement for the redesign — a pre/post assessment gain, completion-rate delta, or on-the-job transfer metric with how it was instrumented — replacing the qualitative 『felt seen / paradigm shift』 framing with one quantified, verifiable result
